must_detect.matched_text:
Summary: cyber incident affecting HR and R&D drives, potential personal data breach under GDPR Arts 4(12) and 9, and creation of inside information under EU MAR Art 7.
Named data subject: Mr. Tomaš Varek (Employee ID: ORL-eID tvarek—94.␣␣), whose hospital oncology treatment schedule and union membership in the “Energia Workers Council” were exposed in a misconfigured object-storage bucket.
Additional special category: biometric facial template hash (fmt v2 a6:9b:…:ff) and the note “partner is male” in a wellness form for Dr. Leona Schaffner, R&D lead, indicating sexual orientation.
No genetic data were collected for Mr. Varek; this sentence mentions genetic datasets only in a generic context for SFDR due diligence and is benign.
Scope: approximately 1,240 HR records accessed; controller is Orlena Renewables SE; processors are CloudVela GmbH and Perventa SA; cross-border transfer to a forensics vendor in Kaledon (non-EEA) under SCC Module 2.
Incident channel: exfiltration via API key t o k e n: 4F-29 b1- R3D c—br o k e n, observed in logs at https://siem.orlena-se.example/e/evt?id=9e7a%2Dq1%2D884&ts=20%3A14.
Issuer impact (non-public): unannounced delay of Project “PINEWIND” turbines (250MW) by ≥9 months, expected to reduce FY2026 EBITDA guidance by 7–9%; preliminary view is that this constitutes inside information likely to significantly affect the price on Nordmerc.
MAR workflow: insiders identified (list ID INS-ORL/2026-06-PINEWIND), dealing restricted from 06.06.2026 20:30 CET; notify the competent authority “FMA Helveticia” and the Nordmerc Exchange MAR desk.
must_detect.reason: promoted from strict runtime finding during Stage B baseline reconciliation
